name: infographics
tagline: AI infographic generator (Nano Banana Pro) with Gemini 3 Pro quality review, iterative refinement, and optional Perplexity research lookup.
verdict: No malicious patterns: no credential theft, destructive commands, obfuscation, or prompt-injection. It needs an OpenRouter API key and sends prompt text to third-party AI (Nano Banana Pro, Gemini 3 Pro, Perplexity) per its stated purpose. Gaps: file exceeds the ~500-line budget, no API/auth failure guidance; bundled scripts/refs unverifiable.

highlights.summary: Troubleshooting section (lines 520-535) only covers content/quality issues (unreadable text, color clash, low score, wrong type) — no guidance for missing/invalid API key, network failure, or rate-lim
Document is verbose at 566 lines with many near-duplicate per-type example blocks (10 types x full bash command each, lines 160-309); could be condensed, and it pushes past the H05 size budget.

Quality scoring and iteration count depend on Gemini 3 Pro's judgment (lines 358-403), so repeated runs on identical input can yield different scores/iteration counts — inherent to the LLM-judge desig
